methods.name: DIFFCOT
methods.description: Diffusion-styled Chain of Thought framework that models CoT reasoning in a diffusion paradigm with trajectory-level revision

methods.name: Step-level Forward Noising Process
methods.description: Noising process for CoT that operates at the step level using reward-ranked candidates

methods.name: Diffusion Sliding Window
methods.description: Window-based mechanism to iteratively denoise past steps while generating new ones during CoT reasoning

methods.name: Causal Diffusion Noise
methods.description: Noise scheduling mechanism that strengthens causal consistency across reasoning steps

methods.name: Reward-ranked Candidate Collection
methods.description: Collection of reasoning candidates ranked by reward scores for use in the noising process

problems.name: Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
problems.description: Step-by-step intermediate reasoning in LLMs for mathematical problem-solving

problems.name: Error Accumulation in CoT
problems.description: Mistakes introduced in early stages of reasoning propagating through later steps leading to incorrect final answers

problems.name: Exposure Bias in Autoregressive Decoding
problems.description: Train/test distribution mismatch where model is exposed only to correct prefixes during training but may condition on erroneous prefixes at inference

problems.name: Lack of Global Consistency in Multi-step Reasoning
problems.description: Step-wise optimization focusing on local alignment and ignoring future signals within the reasoning trajectory

problems.name: Inference-time Overhead of Search-based Methods
problems.description: Substantial computational cost of methods using external critics or search procedures like MCTS

relations.evidence: we propose Diffusion-styled Chain of Thought (DIFFCOT), which models CoT reasoning in a diffusion-styled framework
We first present a step-level forward noising process for CoT using reward-ranked candidates (§3.1)
then apply a diffusion sliding window to iteratively denoise past steps while generating new ones (§3.2)
We further introduce causal diffusion noise to strengthen causal consistency across reasoning steps (§3.3)
step-level forward noising process for CoT using reward-ranked candidates
We first present a step-level forward noising process for CoT using reward-ranked candidates (§3.1)
then apply a diffusion sliding window to iteratively denoise past steps while generating new ones (§3.2)
We further introduce causal diffusion noise to strengthen causal consistency across reasoning steps (§3.3)

intermediate steps are not fixed once generated but can be adaptively corrected in light of later context
Rather than defining reasoning as a strictly forward, prefix-conditioned process...our method jointly models all reasoning steps within a diffusion paradigm

relations.object_name: Lack of Global Consistency in Multi-step Reasoning
relations.evidence: effective CoT reasoning requires trajectory-level revision to enforce global consistency and recover from corrupted intermediate steps
beyond the substantial inference-time overhead of MCTS, these methods share a fundamental limitation that motivates rethinking of the CoT paradigm
CoT reasoning is highly sensitive to potential errors, where mistakes introduced in the early stages can propagate through later steps
exposure bias that commonly plagues autoregressive generation and imitation learning

relations.subject_name: Lack of Global Consistency in Multi-step Reasoning
relations.evidence: step-wise optimization focuses on local alignment and ignores future signals within the reasoning trajectory
